---
title: "SkeletonizeMask (TransformsV2)"
---

## Overview

`SkeletonizeMask` is a V2 element-level transform that reduces a binary mask to a
single-pixel-wide skeleton.

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Registry name | `SkeletonizeMask` |
| Element signature | `Mask2D → Mask2D` |
| Container lift | `MaskData → MaskData` |
| Context variant | `SkeletonizeMaskWithContext` |

Source files:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/MaskSkeletonize/MaskSkeletonize.hpp` | Params + API |
| `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/MaskSkeletonize/MaskSkeletonize.cpp` | Implementation |
| `src/DataObjects/Masks/utils/skeletonize.cpp` | Zhang–Suen thinning |
| `src/DataObjects/Masks/utils/medial_axis_skeletonize.cpp` | Medial-axis skeleton |
| `src/DataObjects/Masks/utils/distance_transform.cpp` | Felzenszwalb EDT |
| `src/TransformsV2/core/RegisteredTransforms.cpp` | Static registration |

## Algorithm

For each input `Mask2D`:

1. Resolve canvas size from `MaskSkeletonizeParams` when both dimensions are positive
   (e.g. copied from `MaskData::getImageSize()`), otherwise size the canvas to the mask's
   axis-aligned point extents via `get_bounding_box`, plus a one-pixel background border
   on each side so thinning is not biased at the raster edge.
2. Rasterize mask points to a binary image on the chosen canvas.
3. Apply the selected skeletonization method (see below).
4. Convert skeleton pixels back to absolute mask coordinates.
5. Return empty `Mask2D` if input is empty or skeletonization yields no pixels.

### Methods

| `method` | Algorithm | Best for |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| `ZhangSuen` (default) | Morphological thinning (`fast_skeletonize`, scikit-image) | Fast, general-purpose thinning |
| `MedialAxis` | Euclidean distance transform (Felzenszwalb) + single-pass medial-axis thinning (scikit-image) | Thick, staggered, or off-center masks where a geometric centerline is desired |

Medial-axis skeletonization is typically more centered on thick bars than Zhang–Suen, which
can hug the top edge of staggered rectangular shapes. For jagged whisker-like masks, chain
with `MedianFilterMask` first (`window_size: 3`) to reduce boundary noise before skeletonizing.
Note that medial axis may still produce short side branches; see scikit-image guidance to
prefer `ZhangSuen` when fewer branches matter more than centering.

## Parameters

```cpp
enum class SkeletonizeMethod {
    ZhangSuen,
    MedialAxis,
};

struct MaskSkeletonizeParams {
    SkeletonizeMethod method = SkeletonizeMethod::ZhangSuen;
    int image_width = -1;   // <= 0 → tight bbox from mask points
    int image_height = -1;  // <= 0 → tight bbox from mask points
};
```

Empty JSON `{}` uses `ZhangSuen` on a tight canvas. Example with medial axis on a full canvas:

```json
{
  "method": "MedialAxis",
  "image_width": 640,
  "image_height": 480
}
```

## Usage

### Direct element call

```cpp
MaskSkeletonizeParams params;
params.method = SkeletonizeMethod::MedialAxis;
auto skeleton = skeletonizeMask(mask, params);
```

### Pipeline

```cpp
TransformPipeline pipeline;
pipeline.addStep("SkeletonizeMask", params);
auto view = pipeline.executeAsViewTyped<MaskData, Mask2D>(mask_data);
```

### DataManager JSON (V2)

```json
{
  "steps": [{
    "step_id": "1",
    "transform_name": "SkeletonizeMask",
    "input_key": "input_masks",
    "output_key": "skeletonized_masks",
    "parameters": {
      "method": "MedialAxis"
    }
  }]
}
```

## Testing

Tests live in:

- `MaskSkeletonize.test.cpp` — V2 transform, canvas modes, Zhang vs MedialAxis on `mask_207252`
- `distance_transform.test.cpp`, `medial_axis_skeletonize.test.cpp` — low-level algorithms

Run via `ctest --preset my-clang-release -R "mask_skeletonize|distance_transform|medial_axis"`.
